dropImages: Drop images here or click to select
supportedFormats: Supports JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF (first frame only)
animatedNotice: Animated images: only the first frame will be used
largeImageNotice: Image exceeds 50MP and may be slow to process

descriptions.title: About Images to PDF
descriptions.aeoDefinition: Images to PDF is a free online tool that converts multiple images into a single PDF document. Supports various layout modes including fit-to-page, grid, and fill page options.
descriptions.whatIs: Convert JPG, PNG, WebP, and GIF images into a single PDF file. Choose from multiple layout modes, page sizes, and margin settings. All processing runs entirely in your browser.
descriptions.stepsTitle: How to Convert Images to PDF
descriptions.step1Text: Drag and drop or click to select multiple images. Supported formats: JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF.
descriptions.step2Text: Choose page size (A4, Letter, or Auto), orientation, layout mode, margins, and alignment.
descriptions.step3Text: Click Generate PDF to create your document. Reorder images by dragging thumbnails before generating.
descriptions.faq1A: JPG, PNG, WebP, and GIF are supported. For animated GIF/WebP, only the first frame is used. WebP and GIF are automatically converted to PNG during processing.
descriptions.faq2Q: Is there a limit on the number of images?
descriptions.faq2A: There is no hard limit. All processing happens in your browser, so the practical limit depends on your device's available memory. Virtual scrolling keeps the UI responsive even with hundreds of images.
descriptions.faq3A: A4 (210 × 297mm), US Letter (8.5 × 11 inches), and Auto which matches the page size to your first image dimensions.
